title: rosa stylosa subsp. rusticana
intro: Rosa stylosa subsp. rusticana, also known as the wild rose or rustic rose, is a species of rose that is native to Australia and New Zealand. It is a thorny shrub with fragrant, pink or white flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa stylosa subsp. rusticana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
